name: First Farmers Bank & Trust Co.
description_short: Community bank established in 1885 offering personal banking, business accounts, agricultural lending, and online/mobile banking services across Indiana and Illinois.
description_long:
First Farmers Bank & Trust Co. was founded in 1885 by Mark Tully, an Irish immigrant who established the Mark Tully Exchange Bank in Converse, Indiana. The bank has grown over 140 years to become a regional financial institution serving local families, farms, and businesses with a focus on community relationships and personalized service.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of financial services including personal checking and savings accounts, business accounts with customized solutions, agricultural lending programs tailored to farm operations, mobile and online banking platforms, and expert financial advice for various life stages. They position themselves as a full-service community bank rather than a specialized lender.

First Farmers distinguishes itself through long-standing customer relationships (some spanning 20+ years), highly-rated customer service across multiple branches, strong online and mobile banking platforms described as user-friendly, and active community engagement through their CRA Spark Program (which donated $149,998+ and contributed 998+ volunteer hours in 2024). The bank emphasizes local decision-making and staff longevity, with testimonials noting many employees have worked there for years.

As a traditional community bank, First Farmers is best suited for customers seeking personalized service and local banking relationships rather than those seeking specialized financial products or promotional rates. The website does not disclose specific product terms, APRs, fees, or competitive rate information, making direct comparison with other banks difficult. Customers needing specialized services like credit repair, debt relief, or non-traditional lending products should look elsewhere.
services: In-person customer service at multiple branch locations
Change conversion and cash handling services
pros: 140-year operating history with established community reputation since 1885
Comprehensive online and mobile banking platform described as user-friendly with secure messaging and timely support responses
Agricultural lending programs specifically designed for farm operations
Multiple branch locations with consistent staff retention and personalized service
Active community investment through CRA Spark Program ($149,998+ donated in 2024)
Strong customer satisfaction reflected in Google reviews praising staff friendliness and professionalism
Full-service banking offering personal accounts, business accounts, and financial advisory services
cons: Website lacks specific product details including APRs, fees, minimum balances, or rate information for competitive comparison
Limited geographic footprint (Indiana and Illinois only) compared to national banks
No information about deposit insurance limits, product warranties, or regulatory compliance details
Marketing-focused website content lacks substantive information about specific account types or lending products
No mention of special promotional offers, new customer incentives, or competitive rate advantages
best_for: Customers in Indiana and Illinois seeking personalized community banking relationships
Agricultural businesses and farm operations needing specialized lending solutions
Customers who value long-term banking relationships and staff familiarity over promotional rates
diagnosis: First Farmers Bank & Trust is best for regional customers in Indiana and Illinois who prioritize personalized service, long-term banking relationships, and community connection over shopping for competitive rates. The primary caveat is the lack of transparent pricing and product information on their website, requiring potential customers to visit a branch or call directly to compare specific account terms, fees, and rates with competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
